'''Darren Shaw''' (born 5 October 1971) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]. He has previously coached the [[Rochdale Hornets]].

Before his coaching career, Shaw played for a number of clubs in England and Australia.  Shaw played for [[Brisbane Broncos]]{{Citation needed|date=August 2008}}, [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Castleford Tigers]],<ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=109|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2013|access-date=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120615054725/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=109|archive-date=15 June 2012 }}</ref> [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Oldham RLFC]] and [[Rochdale Hornets]].

He played {{rlp|sr}} in Sheffield Eagles' 17–8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998.

Darren Shaw was assistant coach to [[Bobbie Goulding]] at [[Rochdale Hornets]] prior to taking charge at Leigh in 2006. Shaw replaced [[Tony Benson (rugby league)|Tony Benson]] after Benson was relieved of his duties following a shock play-off defeat by [[Batley Bulldogs]].

Shortly after Shaw's appointment it was found that he did not have the relevant coaching qualifications to take charge of a team at [[British rugby league system#History|National Leagues]] level.  To counter this, Shaw was made Football Manager, with Paul Rowley becoming First Team coach.

Darren Shaw's first full season in charge of [[Leigh Centurions]] ended with a 19–6 play off defeat away to Whitehaven.  Leigh's 2007 season was one dogged by inconsistency.  Convincing wins were often followed by heavy defeats as Shaw was forced to field a number of youngsters due to injury problems.  The unsettled nature of the side in 2007 was reflected by the fact that Shaw gave débuts to over 20 players over the course of the season.

Despite the disappointing end to the 2007 season, Shaw was given a contract to coach Leigh for the 2008 season.

After a promising start to the 2008 season, Shaw tendered his resignation following defeats by Salford, Widnes and Featherstone Rovers.<ref name=" Aussie Shaw quits as Leigh coach">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7336256.stm|title= Aussie Shaw quits as Leigh coach|date=2008-04-08|access-date=2008-04-08|work=BBC News}}</ref>

He was appointed as head coach of [[Rochdale Hornets]] in May 2008.

==Challenge Cup Final appearances==
Dale Laughton played at {{rlp|sr}} in Sheffield Eagles' 17–8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
